EBay Inc's plans break into three different companies could accommodate would-be suitors, signaling potential merger fight after breakup.
company plans spin payments division, PayPal, from core marketplace division second half year, making standalone publicly traded companies that some analysts could worth more than combined entity.On Wednesday, eBay added that will sell prepare public offering eBay Enterprise unit, which company bought $2.4 billion roughly four years ago.The announced moves intended give each business ability consider their alternatives, including sale, eBay Chief Executive Officer John Donahoe said.
knows what's going happen down road," Donahoe said interview Wednesday, after eBay reported fourth-quarter earnings.

HONG KONG, February 2015 FedEx Express, world's largest express transportation company subsidiary FedEx Corp. (NYSE: FDX), today unveiled results research study into global consumer behavior around cross-border e-commerce.
Conducted independent research firm Forrester Consulting, FedEx-commissioned study reveals marked reluctance among online shoppers Asia-Pacific region (APAC) directly from websites small- medium-sized enterprise (SME) online retailers: APAC respondents ranked this their least-preferred business type cross-border shopping*, ranking below multi-brand online marketplaces, brand manufacturer websites bricks-and-mortar retailers.
study completed September 2014.
surveyed 9006 global online shoppers countries regions, including Australia, China, Hong Kong, Japan, Singapore South Korea Asia Pacific.
study suggests that doubts about using online retailers' websites haven't dampened APAC consumers' appetites cross-border online purchases.
fact, APAC consumers spend average almost US$350 year such purchases, compared global average US$300*.
Consumers certain APAC markets spend even more: mainland China respondents reported spending more than US$1,000 year, example**.
